tasks_completed.changes: Added match_sentinel_grant_for_write(task_id, session_id, target_path) at line 484 (before consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result). Function mirrors match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() shape: calls load_sentinel_grant_for_task, validates grant['session_id'] == session_id, iterates allowed_operations[] for op=='Write' AND entry['target']==target_path (exact equality). Uses 'target' field name per existing schema (allowlist.py:22). Returns matched dict entry or None.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: match_sentinel_grant_for_write() was entirely absent from allowlist.py; the sentinel-grant system only had Bash support. Architectural gap introduced deliberately as deferred scope in task 20260519-211515.

tasks_completed.description: Hoist FILE_PATH/task_id parsing and add sentinel check to pretool-write-guard.sh
tasks_completed.changes: Hoisted FILE_PATH extraction and _WG_TASK_ID resolution to before the _WG_IS_SUB check (was at line 50, after the /allow block). Added sentinel check inside _WG_IS_SUB != '1' block: if sentinel exists for task_id, call match_sentinel_grant_for_write via inline Python; if match -> exit 0; if sentinel exists-but-no-match -> exit 2 immediately (CF-1 invariant — suppresses legacy read_grant('Write') fallback). Legacy /allow block only reached when no sentinel exists.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: write-guard had no sentinel check path and FILE_PATH was parsed after the /allow block (so any sentinel check placed before /allow would lack the file path). Codex findings #3 (parse ordering) and #5 (CF-1 invariant) both incorporated.

tasks_completed.description: Extend posttool-allowlist-consume.py with Write sentinel consumption branch
tasks_completed.changes: Added top-level import of match_sentinel_grant_for_write. Added elif tool_name == 'Write': branch parallel to the existing if task_id and tool_name == 'Bash': block. Write branch extracts file_path = (data.get('tool_input') or {}).get('file_path', ''), calls match_sentinel_grant_for_write(task_id, session_id, file_path), and on match calls consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result(). Malformed-grant fallback also applied for Write. Uses file_path not command (Write has no command field).
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: posttool-allowlist-consume.py:105 gated sentinel consumption exclusively on tool_name == 'Bash'. Codex finding #4 (posttool must use file_path not command) incorporated.

tasks_completed.changes: Added match_sentinel_grant_for_write to imports. Added TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite class with 5 test cases: match_returns_entry, session_mismatch_returns_none, target_mismatch_returns_none, expired_sentinel_returns_none, op_mismatch_returns_none. All 5 pass.
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec S1 (Should Have): test coverage for match_sentinel_grant_for_write.

tasks_completed.description: Implement AC test bodies in tests/generated/20260522-080646-B/
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced TEST_INCOMPLETE stubs with real test bodies. AC1: match/session-mismatch/target-mismatch/expired cases. AC2: bash smoke tests via subprocess (sentinel-match exit 0, CF-1 no-match exit 2, legacy-only exit 0, no-grant exit 2). AC3: posttool Write success/failure sentinel consumption via subprocess. AC4: git diff HEAD scope integrity check.
tasks_completed.rationale: Manifest requires active tests to pass. All 4 AC tests now pass.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — architectural gap (sentinel-grant system task 20260519-211515 deferred Write support as CF2-10 OBSERVATION_ONLY)
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Sentinel-grant system was designed for Bash commands only; Write-tool sentinel support was explicitly deferred as out-of-scope in the prior cycle. No match function existed in allowlist.py, no sentinel check existed in write-guard.sh, and posttool restricted sentinel consumption to tool_name == 'Bash' only.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Added match_sentinel_grant_for_write() to allowlist.py; added sentinel check before the existing-file block in write-guard.sh; extended posttool to consume Write sentinel grants. All three structural absences are now filled.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Three production files each require ~20-40 lines of additive change (not reducible: each implements a distinct path in a separate file). The test additions (test_allowlist_consolidation.py +102 lines) are the dominant contributor — required by BA spec S1 and manifest. The 200-line total reflects 4 file changes across 3 production files + 1 test file with 5 unit test cases.

dev.qa_notes: Run 'python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260522-080646-B/ hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py -v' to verify all 44 tests pass. Key behavioral checks: (1) AC2 smoke test verifies CF-1 invariant: sentinel-exists-but-no-match suppresses legacy /allow grant and exits 2. (2) AC3 verifies sentinel is unlinked after Write PostToolUse. (3) Subagent exemption preserved: sentinel check only fires when _WG_IS_SUB != '1'. (4) hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh not modified (grant creation path unchanged).

tests: AC2 smoke test (4 cases via subprocess)
AC3 smoke test (Write success, Write failure, Bash-with-Write-sentinel)
TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite (5 unit test cases in test_allowlist_consolidation.py)

implementation_notes: CF-1 invariant implemented in write-guard.sh: sentinel existence check via 'ls ${_WG_SENTINEL_DIR}/${_WG_TASK_ID}*.json | grep -q .' — if sentinel exists for the task but does NOT match the write target, the hook exits 2 without falling through to the legacy read_grant('Write') path.
The posttool Write branch is an elif (not a separate if) to prevent double-consumption when Bash and Write are both tool_name == same event. Each PostToolUse event has exactly one tool_name.

AC4 scope check uses hooks/-scoped diff assertion rather than full diff assertion because there are pre-existing uncommitted changes to INDEX.md and test_ac*.sh files from prior cycles — these are not scope violations for this cycle.

self_verification.notes: All 4 AC tests pass. All 40 test_allowlist_consolidation.py tests pass (40 total including 5 new). Syntax check: allowlist.py OK, posttool OK, write-guard bash -n OK.

recommendations: Update /allow command (hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh) to support Write operation grants in the interactive consent flow — currently users cannot create Write sentinel grants without manual JSON editing. This is out-of-scope for this cycle per WN1.
Consider adding a CLAUDE.md note documenting the sentinel-grant Write bypass pattern so future dev cycles know the mechanism exists.
